WordPress LibScript Module

This module installs WordPress alongside a webserver and MariaDB database. It natively supports deploying to Nginx, Caddy, or HTTPD on POSIX systems, and Microsoft IIS on Windows.

Environment Variables

  • WORDPRESS_VERSION: Specific WordPress version to install (default latest).
  • WORDPRESS_WEBSERVER: One of nginx (default on Linux/macOS), caddy, httpd, or iis (default on Windows).
  • WORDPRESS_SERVER_NAME: The FQDN for the application (default localhost).
  • WORDPRESS_LISTEN: The port to listen on (default 80).
  • WWWROOT: The directory to install to (default /var/www/wordpress or C:\inetpub\wwwroot\wordpress).
  • WORDPRESS_DB_NAME, WORDPRESS_DB_USER, WORDPRESS_DB_PASS: Database credentials.
  • PHP_FPM_LISTEN: Unix socket or host:port for PHP-FPM connection (auto-detected if unset).

OS Compatibility & Packaging

This module adheres to LibScript’s cross-platform conventions: - Safely degrades system-level reloads (e.g. systemctl, mysql) during image build phases, ensuring package_as docker emits a single, functional container. - Uses setup_win.ps1 for clean .msi or .exe Windows Installer generation, leveraging winget and native IIS PowerShell configuration blocks (WebAdministration).

  • VerMan.io – deploy like it's the 1980s
  • #rewriteInC.io
  • offscale.io
